# Questions in Livestream 251

Live Stream #251: Live Streams and Rumors of Live Streams

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:35</a> | Did Satan fall before creation, or when he tempted Adam and Eve? | He said he places Satan's fall in connection with Adam and Eve rather than before the creation of the universe. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=776" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:56</a> | Does Matthew 7:18 deny that people can be complex or produce mixed results? | No. He read the passage as a warning about false teachers and ultimate fruit, not as a denial that human behavior can be mixed or complicated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=1015" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:55</a> | What state would the Ark of the Covenant have been in by the time of Josiah? | Probably still serviceable. He said ritual furniture could endure for centuries if it was maintained and protected from bad conditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=1526" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:26</a> | How should we understand Judges 1:8 given other texts and the archaeological questions around Jerusalem? | He treated it as compatible with a limited attack or burn episode without requiring full, permanent Judahite control at that moment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=1871" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:11</a> | Did Egyptians charge Semitic Asiatics fees to graze or water livestock in the Delta? | Not to current knowledge. He said they may have taxed cattle at some point, but there is no evidence for a water or grazing fee. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=1917" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:57</a> | Did people really make a paint called mummy brown from ground-up ancient mummies? | Yes. He said Europeans used ground mummies for pigment and other strange purposes, though the color itself was ugly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=2128" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:28</a> | How effective were Egyptian forts in Sinai for detecting enemies or supplying the army? | Very effective. He said they helped detect and resist attacks and also became part of Egyptian military supply lines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=2256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:36</a> | Is one problem with Esther that much Persian history comes through Greek sources because Persian records are scarce? | Yes. He said Persian records often used perishable materials, so far fewer contemporary Persian sources survived. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=2368" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:28</a> | How do you fit all the judges into the time between conquest and monarchy? | He said everyone has to reckon with overlapping judgeships and regional chronology, because simply adding all the years together does not work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=2511" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:51</a> | Is the Euphrates drying up in Revelation 16:12 literal? | Not necessarily. He treated it as possible but not required, since apocalyptic imagery often communicates more than flat literal prediction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=2768" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:08</a> | How do you understand Jeremiah 3:16, especially with modern claims about rebuilding an ark? | He read Jeremiah as looking beyond reliance on the ark itself, toward a future where God's presence is no longer centered on that object. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=3268" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:28</a> | Will you respond to Holy Kool-Aid? | No. He said he would not respond. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=3318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:18</a> | Why was Gezer targeted so often by invading armies? | Because of its strategic position in the Shephelah and the routes it controlled, not because it was uniquely symbolic on its own. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=3483" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:03</a> | When was Daniel written? | He leaned early rather than late, though without claiming absolute certainty, and at minimum placed it before the fourth century BC. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=3713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:53</a> | Why is the late date for Daniel the consensus view? | He said the consensus is driven largely by scholars who reject predictive prophecy and therefore push Daniel down into the Maccabean period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=4014" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:54</a> | Before the Dead Sea Scrolls, did secular scholars think prophecies like Isaiah 53 and Psalm 22 were written after Jesus? | He said that was not generally the claim; skeptics more often argued that the Gospels were shaped to fit those older texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=4193" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:53</a> | In 2 Samuel 20:4-10, did Joab think Amasa was a traitor? | Yes. He said Amasa's delay would look like treachery in David's weakened political position after Absalom's rebellion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=4549" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:49</a> | What does `priest to the king` mean in 2 Samuel 20:26? | He took it as a court office, essentially a chief royal minister or high-ranking servant, not a second cultic priesthood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=5151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:51</a> | Who is Gog in Ezekiel: a human figure or a fallen angel? | He leaned toward a human or geopolitical ruler-symbol rather than a fallen angel, though he said the issue is debated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=5259" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:39</a> | Is Goshen just a Hebrew name, or is the toponym used in Egypt? | He said an Egyptian form, Geshem or Gesem, appears once and refers to a region rather than a city. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=5360" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:20</a> | Is the Satan in Adam and Eve the same as the Satan in Job? | He said maybe, but there is not enough information about Job's Satan to be certain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=5449" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:49</a> | What is Rizpah doing in 2 Samuel 21:10? | He read it as a mourning and protest act tied to covenant violation, public shame, and the refusal to let the dead remain dishonored. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=6233" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:53</a> | Is the Goliath in 2 Samuel 21:19 the same Goliath? | No. He said Goliath was a reused local name and this was a different Goliath, apparently connected to the original giant's line. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=6678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:18</a> | Are Buddhists really as pacifistic as many Westerners claim? | No. He said the pacifist image is overstated and that Buddhist sects have had serious sectarian violence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=6751" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:31</a> | What is a Repha in 1 Samuel 21-22? | He said `Repha` means a giant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=6936" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:36</a> | How can David say he kept God's ways in 2 Samuel 22:22 if David sinned? | He said kings are judged on covenant faithfulness rather than sinless perfection, and David broadly kept the covenant despite real sins. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=7380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:00</a> | What does Jesus mean in Luke 16:9 about making friends by means of mammon? | He said the point is faithful, shrewd stewardship: use worldly resources righteously rather than dishonestly, because small stewardship reveals larger fidelity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=8402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:02</a> | What happens to a nation's prince or spiritual power when that nation is politically defeated? | He said not much happens to the being itself; it simply continues under the next regime or political structure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=8611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:31</a> | Has the prince of Greece been active through the Byzantine era into the modern republic? | He said yes, because such beings do not die physically and can continue through later political forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=8679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:39</a> | Can principalities expand their domains at the expense of other entities through imperialism? | Yes. He said they can, though defeated powers may move into regional politics or lose status rather than vanish. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=8794" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:34</a> | Did *The Screwtape Letters* explain demonic competitiveness well? | He thought that part was accurate: they cooperate in opposing the gospel but compete over methods and influence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=8862" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:42</a> | What about countries recently created after independence from another nation or empire? | He described that as a power redistribution when empires break apart and principalities divide the resulting domains. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=8946" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:06</a> | Outside of shawls, what is going on in 1 Corinthians 11:14-15, and does it apply today? | He said the core principle is piety, humility, and modesty in worship, while the hair and covering symbols are culturally relative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=9274" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:34</a> | How can someone make video games with no experience? | He said to learn programming, start simple, and consider an accessible engine such as Ren'Py. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=9498" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:18</a> | Can Satan punish other spirit beings for disobeying him, and do they have to answer to him? | Probably he can punish, but they do not owe him true loyalty and do not simply function like a neat chain of command. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=9794" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:14</a> | How do orthopraxic Shinto and orthodox religion compare? | He defined orthopraxy as right practice and orthodoxy as right belief, treating Shinto more as civil religion than strict doctrine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=10311" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:51</a> | Does Bushido come from Shinto or Japanese variants of Buddhism? | He said Bushido was its own cultural movement from Japan's feudal wars, though it interacted with Shinto and Buddhism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=10418" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:38</a> | What happens when a nation turns Christian? Do saints replace its spiritual rulers? | No. He said the principalities are not replaced by saints in that simple way, even when a society becomes Christianized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=10510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:10</a> | Could twentieth-century independence movements be lesser princes carving out domains? | He said it is possible, depending on who is in control and how the political powers are being reorganized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=10705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:25</a> | Is the Niqmaddu of Ugarit and Suppiluliuma II synchronism an error in your chronology? | He said no; the apparent issue comes from looking at the mean dates rather than the full plus-or-minus range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=11176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:16</a> | Do you stand by a 15-year reign length for Seti I? | Yes. He said the shorter 9-11 year view mishandles the proposed co-regency with Ramesses II. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=11714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:14</a> | How do theories about early matrilineal societies relate to the sons of God and daughters of men in Genesis 6? | He did not see a direct relationship and instead read Genesis 6 as ritual language about priestly lines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=12011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:11</a> | Is an early matrilineal society consistent with Genesis, especially Adam and Eve? | No. He said Genesis and Old Testament genealogies are overwhelmingly patrilineal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=12108" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:48</a> | Where does Scripture mention rebellions before Satan? | He pointed to Jude 6, reading it through ancient Near Eastern categories rather than as Genesis 6 angel-human relations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=12476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:56</a> | Why does the beast turn against the great prostitute in Revelation 17:16? | He read it as Satanic power eventually turning on the very false system it used, because evil has no real loyalty even to its own instruments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=12916" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:16</a> | Does the devil create false religions to distract from God? | Yes, but he said Satan has no real respect or love for the false systems and people he deceives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=13284" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:24</a> | Does Revelation about the beast apply right now or in the future? | He leaned futurist, saying Revelation clarifies Old Testament prophecies that have not yet fully come to pass. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jwv2y8JJ7as?t=13386" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:06</a> | What could Adam technically do to the serpent instead of Eve? | He found the question unclear, but emphasized that Adam was present, was not deceived, and knowingly disobeyed. |
